Dr. Seuss’ The Lorax (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $214,151,550Details
International Box Office $136,946,253
Worldwide Box Office $351,097,803
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $84,823,863
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $31,625,478
Total $116,449,341
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Dr. Seuss’ The Lorax is a 3D-CG adaptation of the classic tale of a forest guardian who shares the enduring power of hope. The animated adventure follows the journey of a 12-year-old as he searches for a real Truffula Tree, the one thing that will enable him to win the affection of the girl of his dreams. To get it he must find the story of the Lorax, the acerbic yet charming character who fights to protect his world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$70,217,070 (32.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.05 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:61.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$67,500,000 (worldwide box office is 5.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,729 opening theaters/3,769 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $321,715,855

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 2nd, 2012 (Wide) by Universal, released as Dr. Seuss' The Lorax
March 2nd, 2012 (IMAX) by Universal, released as Dr. Seuss' The Lorax
International Releases: March 1st, 2012 (Wide), released as Doctor Seuss' The Lorax (United Arab Emirates)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide), released as Doctor Seuss' The Lorax (Singapore)
March 9th, 2012 (Wide), released as Doctor Seuss' The Lorax (Latvia)
March 9th, 2012 (Wide), released as Doctor Seuss' The Lorax (Poland)
March 14th, 2012 (Wide), released as Doctor Seuss' The Lorax (Egypt)
Video Release: August 7th, 2012 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for brief mild language.
(Rating bulletin 2206, 1/18/2012)
Running Time: 101 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Agitprop, Breaking the Fourth Wall, Fantasy Sequence, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Coming of Age, Corporate Malfeasance, Dysfunctional Family, Environment, Government Corruption, Inventor, Non-Chronological, Romance
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Chris Meledandri, Illumination Entertainment
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Dr. Seuss’ The Lorax is a 3D-CG adaptation of the classic tale of a forest guardian who shares the enduring power of hope. The animated adventure follows the journey of a 12-year-old as he searches for a real Truffula Tree, the one thing that will enable him to win the affection of the girl of his dreams. To get it he must find the story of the Lorax, the acerbic yet charming character who fights to protect his world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$70,217,070 (32.8% of total gross)
Legs:3.05 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:61.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$67,500,000 (worldwide box office is 5.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,729 opening theaters/3,769 max. theaters, 7.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $321,715,855

March 2nd, 2012 (IMAX) by Universal, released as Dr. Seuss' The Lorax
